content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Patched the two pending failure-reconciliation defects additively. In `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs`, attempt-boundary resolution for `terminal_event_for_attempt`/`terminal_status_for_attempt` now uses the later of the durable and inferred boundaries, so resumed attempts fail closed when `run.json` has a newer `started_at` but `run_resumed` was never durably written. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs`, retry ordinals now derive from terminal failed attempts instead of every known start, while still inferring missing prior failures only for same-`run_id` retries that were reopened in place after a failed partial write. I also added focused regressions for both cases and updated the stale CLI fixture in `src/cli/run.rs` to use an attempt timestamp consistent with its durable `run_failed` event under the stricter boundary logic.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Updated `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs` so attempt-scoped terminal lookups compute the lower boundary as `max(durable_boundary, inferred_boundary)`, preserving the existing fail-closed semantics for resumed attempts whose durable `run_resumed` event is missing.
2. [Completed] Reworked `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs` so retry escalation counts only durable failed attempts plus inferred missing failures from same-`run_id` reopen/retry history, instead of consuming retries for unrelated start-only artifacts.
3. [Completed] Added focused regression coverage for stale resumed-attempt terminal matching and for retry counting that ignores orphaned start-only attempts while preserving same-`run_id` partial-write escalation behavior.
4. [Completed] Updated the legacy CLI failure-sync fixture in `src/cli/run.rs` to use a started-at timestamp aligned with its durable project-journal attempt boundary, then reran the Rust validation suite and relaunched the Nix gate.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- Targeted regressions passed: `cargo test failure_reconciliation -- --nocapture` and `cargo test terminal_attempt_queries_ignore_stale_terminal_events_before_inferred_resume_boundary -- --nocapture`.
- `cargo test` passed on the full suite after updating the stale CLI fixture.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `cargo clippy --features test-stub -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix build` was launched and reached `/nix/store/zrp1wfv673rhdw9rk6vy0mac8c32qz26-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but no final exit status was observed in-session.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` was launched on the updated tree and remained in the active derivation build stage without returning a final success/failure status in-session, so the authoritative Nix gate is still unresolved.
- Pre-existing live orchestration-state edits under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni854-1/` were left untouched and remain out of scope.
